name: Western Sahara (Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic)

colors.symbolism: the Abbasid caliphate; mourning for those killed in the struggle for independence
the Hashemite dynasty; the blood shed for liberation

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of black (top), white and green, with a red equilateral triangle at the hoist. A red crescent and five-pointed star are centred on the white stripe.
history: Adopted by the Polisario Front on 27 February 1976, the day the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic was proclaimed following Spain's withdrawal from Spanish Sahara. The design closely mirrors the Pan-Arab colour scheme used by other Arab liberation movements (Palestine, Jordan, Sudan, Ba'athist flags). The territory's status remains disputed: most of Western Sahara is administered by Morocco, while the SADR controls a smaller eastern strip and is recognised by a number of states and the African Union.

features.symbolism: the Pan-Arab colours used across the Arab world; black, white and green together with red recall the four traditional caliphal dynasties
the blood shed by the Sahrawi people in the struggle for independence
Islam, the religion of the Sahrawi people
Islam and the unity of the Arab nation
